description: Restructure Kami data: store current effects as expansion, replace with base game effects, add base game kami logic function, add i18n for all kami texts
steps: In src/types/game.ts, rename current KAMI_DATA to KAMI_DATA_EXPANSION (keep as export). Create new KAMI_DATA with the same structure but base game effects in Spanish. The base game effects are: Amaterasu: 'Allows worshiper to move clan marker to top of Honor Track.', Fujin: 'Allows worshiper to perform up to 2 Movements with their figures.', Raijin: 'Allows worshiper to Summon 1 Bushi to any Province.', Ryujin: 'Allows worshiper to acquire a Season Card paying full cost.', Hachiman: 'Grants worshiper 2 Ronin tokens.', Susanoo: 'Grants worshiper VP equal to their number of Fortresses on the Map.', Tsukuyomi: 'Grants worshiper 2 Coins.'
Add base game kami effect translation keys to src/i18n/en.ts: 'kami.amaterasu.baseEffect', 'kami.fujin.baseEffect', etc. with the English effect text.

Add base game kami effect translation keys to src/i18n/es.ts with the full Spanish text provided by the user: kami.amaterasu.baseEffect = 'Diosa del Sol, Amaterasu permite a su adorador principal mover su marcador de Clan directamente a la posicion superior del Track de Honor...', etc. for all 7 kami.
Rename existing kami.*.effect keys to kami.*.expansionEffect in both en.ts and es.ts to clearly mark them as expansion effects.

Update any components that reference KAMI_DATA or kami effect translations (src/components/SeasonCardsModal.tsx won't reference kami, but check the kami track/modal components). Update imports in src/utils/gameLogic.ts if needed.

In src/utils/gameLogic.ts, create a new exported function resolveKamiAbility(state: GameState, kamiType: KamiType, playerId: string): GameState that implements the base game kami abilities: amaterasu moves player to top of honorTrack, fujin is a placeholder (movement is UI-driven), raijin summons 1 bushi to any province (pick first province without the player for now, or just decrement reserve), ryujin is a placeholder (buying is UI-driven), hachiman grants +2 ronin, susanoo grants VP equal to fortress count on map, tsukuyomi grants +2 coins.

Update resolveKamiTurn in src/utils/gameLogic.ts to call resolveKamiAbility for each temple winner, applying the actual base game effect (amaterasu, hachiman, susanoo, tsukuyomi can be fully automated; fujin, raijin, ryujin need markers/flags for UI-driven resolution later).
acceptance_criteria: KAMI_DATA contains base game effects, KAMI_DATA_EXPANSION contains the old expansion effects
Both en.ts and es.ts have kami.*.baseEffect and kami.*.expansionEffect keys
resolveKamiAbility function exists and implements at least amaterasu (honor track top), hachiman (+2 ronin), susanoo (VP per fortress), tsukuyomi (+2 coins)
resolveKamiTurn calls resolveKamiAbility for each temple winner
Build passes with npx tsc -b && npx vite build
findings: Successfully restructured Kami data. KAMI_DATA now contains base game effects, KAMI_DATA_EXPANSION stores the old expansion effects. Both en.ts and es.ts have kami.*.baseEffect and kami.*.expansionEffect keys. resolveKamiAbility implements amaterasu (honor track top), hachiman (+2 ronin), susanoo (VP per fortress), tsukuyomi (+2 coins), with placeholders for fujin/raijin/ryujin (UI-driven). resolveKamiTurn now calls resolveKamiAbility for each temple winner. TemplePanel.tsx updated to display baseEffect. Build passes cleanly.
